page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-32GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation, 8 physical cores, and 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or a GPU, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance profile. The server demonstrates poor single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the bottom 10%. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels in stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks registers in the average tier, though the PassMark memory score is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput is also weak, while database operations and Redis SET operations achieve average performance.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and weak single-core metrics, this server is not suited for compute-intensive single-threaded applications or high-throughput web serving. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads that can leverage the 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB memory pool without requiring dedicated CPU resources. Appropriate workloads include general-purpose applications, multi-threaded background processing, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-8xCPU-32GB is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor at 2.0 GHz, it features 8 vCPUs with shared CPU allocation, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM. The server does not include local storage or a GPU. Performance benchmarks show poor single-core CPU performance, but average multi-core scaling and average memory bandwidth. While static web serving and PassMark memory scores are weak, database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and general-purpose tasks that do not require dedicated CPU resources or high single-core performance.
